overview.description: We are delighted to invite your students to participate in the Inter-NIT Project Expo 2025, proudly hosted by NIT Warangal. This prestigious event aims to bring together bright innovators from IITs, NITs, IIITs, and other leading institutes to showcase impactful projects, exchange ideas, and foster meaningful collaborations

title: DevOps Workshop
name: HAM ARC Club

overview.description: Teaching the fundamentals of CI/CD pipelines, automation, version control, and containerization with practical insights into tools and workflows used by top tech companies.

rules: Containerisation- use of docker, docker commands
Small intro to orchestration- use of kubernates

title: MoCap Mirror
name: Painting and Animation Club

overview.description: A unique blend of live tech demonstration and a hands-on interactive workshop. Volunteers perform on stage and their movements are instantly captured and projected as animated 3D characters, letting the audience experience the technology behind movies and games.

rules: see the character copy your movements live on screen for 60-90 seconds

title: Photography Light Painting Session
name: Photography Club

overview.description: An experienced guest photographer will lead a workshop on mobile photography, sharing tips and techniques to capture creative and professional shots using just a smartphone.

rules: Participants must bring their own phones with enough battery and storage.
Each participant will get 3-5 minutes for a joint light painting session with the guest photographer.

overview.description: Learn how to transform your smartphone into a powerful storytelling tool. This hands-on workshop, conducted by a journalist, will guide you through the essentials of mobile reporting, shooting, and editing.
The Innovation Garage is bringing in top experts for a dynamic speaker session to inspire students and provide real-world insights and practical guidance on entrepreneurship and innovation.

title: The LaunchPad
name: SEA Club

overview.description: In this 3D rocket building hackathon, teams from around campus will build a prototype rocket and launch it to win the competition.

rules: Each team will be given a set of designs and models required to build it from scratch.
Hardware parts will be printed prior to the final competition; assembly of electronics and hardware will take place on the day of the event.
